name: Pho Bac
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Hot chili sauce (tuong ot) - or nuoc cham)
recipeInstructions:
1. Freeze the beef pieces for 30 minutes before slicing to ensure paper-thin cuts.
2. The night before, rinse bones in cold water and soak overnight at room temperature to loosen impurities.
3. Boil bones with water to cover, then drain and rinse. Return bones to pot with six quarts of water, bring to boil, skim foam and fat. Continue skimming until no foam rises, add three quarts more water, return to boil, skim residue, turn heat low and simmer.
4. Char clove-studded onions, shallots, ginger directly over gas burner or under broiler until fragrant. Tie charred vegetables, star anise, cinnamon stick in dampened cheesecloth. Add spice bag, parsnips, and salt to simmering broth; simmer 1 hour.
5. Remove short rib plates, reserve meat, return bones to pot. Simmer uncovered for 4-5 hours, adding water as needed.
6. Slice beef sirloin and reserved short rib meat paper-thin. In small bowl combine scallions, coriander, half sliced onions; stir hot chili sauce in separate bowl.
7. Soak rice sticks in warm water 30 minutes, drain.
8. When broth ready, strain through cheesecloth-lined strainer into clean pot. Add fish sauce, bring to boil, reduce heat, keep simmering.
9. Boil four quarts of water for noodles, drop drained noodles in boiling water, then drain immediately. Divide noodles among bowls. Top with sliced meats; return broth to boil, ladle over meat (boiling broth cooks raw beef instantly). Garnish with scallion mixture and black pepper. Serve onions in chili sauce and accompaniments side by side.
